# Vectra

> Vectra is a local, file-backed, in-memory vector database with a gRPC server for cross-language access. Each index is a folder on disk; queries use MongoDB-style metadata filters and cosine similarity ranking. Ideal for small, mostly static corpora: prompt augmentation, few-shot libraries, single-document Q&A, and local dev workflows.

Vectra works like a local Pinecone or Qdrant. Indexes load fully into memory for fast lookups (<1 ms for small indexes, 1-2 ms for medium). It supports two storage formats (JSON default, Protocol Buffers for 40-50% smaller files) and two index types: `LocalIndex` (you supply vectors + metadata) and `LocalDocumentIndex` (Vectra chunks, embeds, and retrieves text).

The built-in gRPC server (`vectra serve`) exposes 19 RPCs so any language can use Vectra as a vector database. Generate idiomatic client scaffolding for Python, C#, Rust, Go, Java, or TypeScript with `vectra generate --language <lang>`.

## Using the TypeScript Library

Install: `npm install vectra`

Key exports: `LocalIndex`, `LocalDocumentIndex`, `OpenAIEmbeddings`, `LocalEmbeddings`, `TransformersEmbeddings`, `TransformersTokenizer`, `JsonCodec`, `ProtobufCodec`, `migrateIndex`, `VectraServer`, `IndexManager`, `FolderWatcher`, `TextSplitter`, `GPT3Tokenizer`, `ItemSelector`, `FileFetcher`, `WebFetcher`, `BrowserWebFetcher`, `LocalFileStorage`, `VirtualFileStorage`, `IndexedDBStorage`, `pathUtils`.

Browser/Electron entry point: `import { ... } from 'vectra/browser'` — excludes Node-specific modules (LocalFileStorage, FileFetcher, WebFetcher, FolderWatcher) and includes browser alternatives (BrowserWebFetcher, IndexedDBStorage). Bundlers with conditional export support auto-resolve `vectra` to the browser entry point when targeting browsers.

Storage backends:
- `LocalFileStorage` — filesystem (Node.js, default)
- `IndexedDBStorage` — IndexedDB (browsers, Electron)
- `VirtualFileStorage` — in-memory (any environment, ephemeral)
- Custom implementations via the `FileStorage` interface

Vectra runs in browsers and Electron using `IndexedDBStorage` for persistence and `TransformersEmbeddings` or `LocalEmbeddings` for offline vector generation.

Embeddings providers:
- `OpenAIEmbeddings` — OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
- `LocalEmbeddings` — local HuggingFace models, no API key (requires optional `@huggingface/transformers`)
- `TransformersEmbeddings` — async factory with device selection (GPU/WASM/CPU), quantization (fp32/fp16/q8/q4), and pooling options. Use `await TransformersEmbeddings.create(options)` to instantiate.

LocalIndex (items + metadata):
```ts
import { LocalIndex } from 'vectra';
const index = new LocalIndex('./my-index');
await index.createIndex({ version: 1, metadata_config: { indexed: ['category'] } });
await index.insertItem({ vector: [0.1, 0.2, ...], metadata: { text: 'hello', category: 'greeting' } });
const results = await index.queryItems(queryVector, '', 3, { category: { $eq: 'greeting' } });
```

LocalDocumentIndex (documents + chunking):
```ts
import { LocalDocumentIndex, OpenAIEmbeddings } from 'vectra';
const embeddings = new OpenAIEmbeddings({ apiKey: '...', model: 'text-embedding-3-small', maxTokens: 8000 });
const docs = new LocalDocumentIndex({ folderPath: './my-doc-index', embeddings });
await docs.createIndex({ version: 1 });
await docs.upsertDocument('doc://readme', text, 'md');
const results = await docs.queryDocuments('search query', { maxDocuments: 5, maxChunks: 20 });
```

Filter operators (Pinecone-compatible subset): `$and`, `$or`, `$eq`, `$ne`, `$gt`, `$gte`, `$lt`, `$lte`, `$in`, `$nin`. Only fields in `metadata_config.indexed` are filterable.

## Using Vectra from Other Languages (gRPC)

Any language can use Vectra via the gRPC server. The workflow is:

1. Start the server: `npx vectra serve ./my-index --keys ./keys.json --port 50051`
2. Generate client bindings: `npx vectra generate --language <lang> --output ./bindings/<lang>`
3. Use the generated `VectraClient` class in your code

Multi-index mode: `npx vectra serve --root ./indexes --keys ./keys.json`
Daemon mode: add `--daemon --pid-file ./vectra.pid`; stop with `npx vectra stop --pid-file ./vectra.pid`

The server binds to 127.0.0.1 only. Clients send text; the server computes embeddings server-side.

gRPC service RPCs (VectraService):
- Index management: `CreateIndex`, `DeleteIndex`, `ListIndexes`
- Item CRUD: `InsertItem`, `UpsertItem`, `BatchInsertItems`, `GetItem`, `DeleteItem`, `ListItems`
- Queries: `QueryItems`, `QueryDocuments`
- Documents: `UpsertDocument`, `DeleteDocument`, `ListDocuments`
- Stats: `GetIndexStats`, `GetCatalogStats`
- Lifecycle: `Healthcheck`, `Shutdown`

Python example:
```python
from vectra_client import VectraClient
client = VectraClient("localhost:50051")
client.create_index("my-index", metadata_config={"indexed": ["category"]})
client.insert_item("my-index", text="hello world", metadata={"category": "greeting"})
results = client.query_items("my-index", text="hi there", top_k=3)
```

C# example:
```csharp
using Vectra.Client;
var client = new VectraClient("localhost:50051");
await client.CreateIndexAsync("my-index", new MetadataConfig { Indexed = ["category"] });
await client.InsertItemAsync("my-index", text: "hello world", metadata: new { category = "greeting" });
var results = await client.QueryItemsAsync("my-index", text: "hi there", topK: 3);
```

Rust example:
```rust
use vectra_client::VectraClient;
let client = VectraClient::connect("http://localhost:50051").await?;
client.create_index("my-index", Some(vec!["category"])).await?;
client.insert_item("my-index", "hello world", json!({"category": "greeting"})).await?;
let results = client.query_items("my-index", "hi there", 3).await?;
```

## CLI Reference

All commands: `npx vectra --help`
Global options: `--storage <local|virtual>`, `--storage-root <path>`

- `vectra create <path> [--format json|protobuf]` — create a new index
- `vectra delete <path>` — delete an index
- `vectra add <path> --keys <keys.json> --uri <url|file>` — add documents
- `vectra remove <path> --uri <uri>` — remove documents
- `vectra query <path> "<text>" --keys <keys.json>` — query an index
- `vectra stats <path>` — print index statistics
- `vectra migrate <path> --to <json|protobuf>` — migrate storage format
- `vectra serve [path] --keys <keys.json> [--root <dir>] [--port 50051] [--daemon]` — start gRPC server
- `vectra stop --pid-file <path>` — stop daemon
- `vectra generate --language <lang> --output <dir>` — generate language bindings
- `vectra watch <index> --keys <keys> --uri <path>... [--list <file>] [--extensions .txt .md] [--debounce 500] [--chunk-size 512]` — watch folders and auto-sync file changes into an index

keys.json for OpenAI: `{ "apiKey": "sk-...", "model": "text-embedding-3-small", "maxTokens": 8000 }`
keys.json for Azure: `{ "azureApiKey": "...", "azureEndpoint": "https://...", "azureDeployment": "...", "azureApiVersion": "2023-05-15" }`

## On-disk Format

Each index is a folder. JSON format (default):
- `index.json` — version, metadata_config, items array (id, vector, norm, metadata, optional metadataFile)
- `*.json` — per-item metadata files
- `catalog.json` — URI-to-ID mapping (document indexes)
- `<documentId>.txt` — document bodies

Protocol Buffer format (`.pb` extensions instead of `.json`). Migrate with `vectra migrate <path> --to protobuf`.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/README.md): Project overview and quick example
- [Getting Started](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): Install, quick start for both index types
- [Core Concepts](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/core-concepts.md): Index types, metadata filtering, on-disk layout
- [Embeddings Guide](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/embeddings.md): Provider comparison, configuration for OpenAI/Azure/Local/Transformers
- [Document Indexing](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/documents.md): Chunking, retrieval, hybrid search, FolderWatcher
- [CLI Reference](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/cli.md): All CLI commands with flags and examples
- [API Reference](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/api-reference.md): TypeScript API overview
- [Best Practices](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/best-practices.md): Performance, troubleshooting
- [Storage](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/storage.md): Pluggable backends, browser/IndexedDB, formats
- [gRPC Server](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/grpc.md): Cross-language access, service API, bindings
- [Changelog](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/changelog.md): Breaking changes, migration guides
- [Tutorials](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/docs/tutorials/): RAG pipeline, custom storage, browser/Electron, gRPC cross-language, folder sync
- [Proto Definition](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/proto/vectra_service.proto): Full gRPC service definition
- [Contributing](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
- [License](https://github.com/Stevenic/vectra/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT License
